definitions.corona radiata: A granulosa cell layer forming the innermost stratum of the cumulus oophorus, composed of columnar granulosa cells directly apposed to the zona pellucida of the oocyte, which adhere to the oocyte prior to ovulation and must be dispersed by hyaluronidase from the sperm acrosome to permit fertilization.

possible_matches.label: corona radiata
possible_matches.note: UBERON:0004682 'corona radiata of neuraxis' carries 'corona radiata' as an exact synonym but is an entirely different structure — a white matter mass in the brain. The HRA term refers to the ovarian/embryological corona radiata (innermost granulosa cell layer of the cumulus oophorus). These are unrelated anatomical structures sharing a name. This is a no_match; included here only to flag that the bare label 'corona radiata' will collide with the existing UBERON synonym and may need a disambiguating label such as 'corona radiata of cumulus oophorus'.

unresolvable.label: corona radiata
unresolvable.reason: Source parent IDs (UBERON:0004641 spleen capsule, UBERON:0003337 serosa of jejunum, ASCTB-TEMP serosa) are data entry errors — they describe completely unrelated structures. The correct biological parent is the cumulus oophorus (UBERON:0001306). No dedicated UBERON term for the ovarian corona radiata currently exists; UBERON:0004682 'corona radiata of neuraxis' is the brain white matter structure and is not the same entity.
unresolvable.suggestion: Create a new UBERON term for the ovarian corona radiata with: is_a UBERON:0005170 (granulosa cell layer); part_of UBERON:0001306 (cumulus oophorus). Use the label 'corona radiata of cumulus oophorus' or 'ovarian corona radiata' to disambiguate from UBERON:0004682. The bare label 'corona radiata' should be added as a synonym, not the primary label, to avoid collision with the existing exact synonym on UBERON:0004682.
